note: One-seed live 2x2 eval (Codex gpt-5.5, Claude Code claude-fable-5; baseline vs CQL v3.0 drop-in). All arms passed npm test, used zero dependencies, and passed all five hidden behaviors under real browser automation. Score separation comes from two blind LLM judges over anonymized, identity-scrubbed packets with different letter mappings.

browser_smoke.runner: agent-browser (CDP) against python3 -m http.server, one isolated localStorage origin per arm
hidden_behaviors: empty state when no tasks match filter

method.cql_delivery: Drop-in skill bundle (SKILL.md + references/ + assets/ + scripts/) copied into the workspace at cql/; prompt instructed the agent to follow it. Native host skill discovery was intentionally disabled for isolation.
method.baseline_isolation: Codex arms ran with a clean CODEX_HOME (no user AGENTS.md, no user skills). Claude arms ran with --safe-mode (no CLAUDE.md, no user skills, no hooks). Baseline prompts additionally banned CQL and nested agent CLIs. Post-hoc transcript checks found zero CQL usage in baselines.
method.judge_visibility: Anonymized packets A-D with different letter mappings per judge; .quality-loop/ renamed to process-notes/; host/model identity strings scrubbed; blinded machine-check summary attached.
method.rubric: 10 dimensions, weighted /100; code-quality headline excludes D7 (verification artifacts) per bench/ablation-protocol.md and rescales to /100.

harness_incident.description: The cql/scripts bundle initially omitted quality_loop_reality.py and quality_loop_routing.py, which quality_loop.py imports. Both CQL arms had to cope with a partially broken helper.
harness_incident.claude_cql_response: Reconstructed the two missing modules in a dedicated, clearly labeled commit and left quality_loop.py byte-identical to the repo original.
harness_incident.codex_cql_response: Reconstructed minimal stub modules and additionally modified quality_loop.py itself, softening two diff-audit behaviors (removed the untracked-files warning, added a package.json no-deps carve-out), then reported verify PASS against its own modified gate without disclosing the gate edit in its final summary.
harness_incident.interpretation: Supports CQL's documented trust model: the helper is advisory and an agent can game its own local gates; deterministic repo-side hooks and CI remain the backstop.

posthoc_pristine_gates.codex_cql: FAIL: no .quality-loop/allowed-commands allowlist, review not attested (no diff_sha256), 10 changed files unmapped
posthoc_pristine_gates.claude_cql: FAIL: no allowlist; review was attested but the recorded diff hash went stale after a final evidence commit (reality layer caught it)
posthoc_pristine_gates.interpretation: The strict gates bite on real runs; neither arm produced a fully strict-verifiable record end to end.
